mnemonics: My Private Key Is My Password, Keep It Secret (M-P-K-I-M-P-K-I-S)
Wallets Don't Hold Coins, They Hold Keys (W-D-H-C-T-H-K)
keyPoints: A wallet generates and stores your public and private cryptographic keys.
The public key (your wallet address) is for receiving funds, like an email address.
The private key is for authorizing transactions, like a password; anyone who has it controls the assets.
Seed phrases (12-24 words) are a human-readable backup of your private key.
Hot wallets are connected to the internet (convenient but less secure), cold wallets are offline (secure but less convenient).
commonMistakes: Not backing up the seed phrase and losing access to the wallet permanently.
Storing a seed phrase or private key digitally (e.g., a screenshot, email) where it can be hacked.
Confusing a wallet address with a private key and sharing the private key by mistake.
Sending crypto assets to a wallet address on the wrong blockchain network (e.g., sending an ETH token to an Ethereum address on the Polygon network).
